title: cercocarpus montanus var. glaber
intro: Cercocarpus montanus var. glaber, also known as the Mountain Mahogany, is a deciduous shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Mountain Mahogany include its ability to treat fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
